title: Everyone Thought the Sun Was Fixed — The Qur'an Said It's Racing Through Space
_note: CINEMATIC, hook-first. Footage = sun/galaxy/stars (no humans; clips thumbnail-verified). NO recital. Simple English. SOURCE: Surah Ya-Sin 36:38 — 'the sun runs to a resting place (mustaqarr) for it.' SCIENCE (solid): the Sun is not fixed — it orbits the Milky Way's center at ~230 km/s (~828,000 km/h), one galactic year ~225-250 million years; the whole solar system also drifts toward the solar apex. Honest framing (the sun genuinely moves; people historically thought it fixed). Safe-zone captions. #HistoryTikTokContest.

segments.text: For thousands of years, humans believed the sun was fixed in the sky. The Quran said something different. And it turned out to be true.
It said the sun is not standing still. It is running, toward a resting place of its own.
For centuries that made no sense. The sun looked perfectly still to everyone.
Then astronomers discovered the truth. The sun is flying through our galaxy at over eight hundred thousand kilometers per hour.
And it is dragging the Earth, and you, along with it, on a lap around the galaxy that takes over two hundred million years.
A book from the seventh century said the sun runs to a destination. Science took thirteen hundred years to catch up.
And the sun runs toward its resting place. That is the design of the Almighty, the All-Knowing.
It was never standing still. And neither are you.

sources: Qur'an, Surah Ya-Sin 36:38 — 'And the sun runs to a resting place for it; that is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.'

Astronomy: the Sun orbits the galactic center at ~230 km/s (~828,000 km/h); one galactic orbit ≈ 225-250 million years; the solar system also moves toward the solar apex. The Sun is not stationary — historically it was thought fixed.
Qur'an 36:38 shown as Arabic + English; not recited.
VISUALS: all Pexels clips verified by thumbnail — no human faces/bodies.
